lines: DAY 2 PROBLEM STATEMENT #03
nn
PolicyPilot
"AI That Navigates Government Schemes So Citizens Don't Have To"
India has 650+ central and state government welfare schemes — but 73% of eligible
citizens never claim them because they cannot figure out eligibility, documentation,
or the application process. Build an agentic AI system where a citizen describes their
situation in plain language or voice, and the agent automatically finds all schemes
they qualify for, generates a step-by-step application checklist, and fills out draft
application forms using their uploaded identity and income documents.
REAL WORLD CONTEXT
NITI Aayog published a report in 2024 identifying this exact gap as a national priority.
MyScheme.gov.in exists but has zero intelligence layer — it is just a search box. The
Union Budget 2025 allocated dedicated funds for AI-powered citizen services. This
solution has a direct, documented path to government adoption.
INPUT
n
Citizen's situation described in plain language or voice (e.g. 'I am a 45-year-old farmer
in Gujarat with 2 acres of land and annual income below 1.5 lakh')
n
Uploaded identity documents: Aadhaar, income certificate, caste certificate (optional)
n
State of residence for filtering central vs state scheme eligibility
n
Specific need category: education, agriculture, health, housing, employment
EXPECTED OUTPUT
n
Full list of schemes the citizen qualifies for — with eligibility match reasoning
n
Step-by-step application checklist for each scheme: documents needed, offices to visit,
deadlines
n
Auto-filled draft application form using details from uploaded documents
n
Conflict flag: if central and state versions of the same scheme have contradicting
criteria, the system must surface and explain the conflict explicitly
n
Plain-language summary in Hindi or the citizen's preferred regional language
THE TWIST
IAR Udaan Hackathon 2026 (cid:127) Day 2 Problem Statement (cid:127) Page 2

IAR Udaan Hackathon 2026 (cid:127) Day 2 Problem Statement (cid:127) Page 2

lines: The agent must use RAG over actual official scheme PDFs — not summarized versions
— and handle conflicting eligibility criteria across central vs state versions of the same
scheme intelligently. When two government documents contradict each other, the agent
must flag the conflict explicitly rather than silently picking one. Hiding contradictions or
hallucinating eligibility criteria is an immediate disqualification. Every scheme
recommendation must cite the exact PDF clause it came from.
DELIVERABLES
n
Working agentic prototype with plain-language or voice input interface
n
RAG pipeline over at least 5 real government scheme PDFs
n
Live demo: describe a citizen profile, show matched schemes + application checklist
n
Conflict detection demo: show the system handling two contradicting scheme clauses
n
Auto-filled draft form output using at least one uploaded document
n
README or 2-minute walkthrough explaining the agentic architecture and retrieval
design
STACK HINT Agentic AI + RAG over government PDF corpus + Multilingual voice input +
Conflict detection + Form auto-fill with document grounding

IAR Udaan Hackathon 2026 (cid:127) Day 2 Problem Statement (cid:127) Page 3

texts: Dharmee Choksi   ·   Vaidik Prajapati   ·   Aena Patel   ·   Vishal Sharma
They don't even know which schemes exist
Eligibility criteria is buried in PDFs
Everything is in English — not everyone reads it
Central and state schemes conflict — nobody flags it
India has 650+ central & state schemes. The problem isn't lack of schemes — it's lack of navigation.
These are documented facts — not assumptions we made up for the hackathon.
NITI Aayog's 2024 report officially flagged the scheme-discovery gap as a national problem that needs fixing urgently. This is the government itself saying the current system isn't working.
The Portal Exists. It Just Has Zero Intelligence.
India already has an official scheme portal — but it's literally just a search box. No eligibility matching, no document guidance, no language support, no conflict detection. Nothing smart.
The Union Budget 2025 specifically allocated funds for AI-powered citizen services. There's a real, documented path from what we're building here to actual government adoption.

Build a system where any citizen — regardless of education level, language, or tech literacy — can describe their situation in plain words and instantly know which government schemes they qualify for, what documents they need, and how to actually apply.
Match citizen profiles to eligible schemes automatically — with reasoning
Generate a step-by-step application checklist for each matched scheme
Auto-fill draft application forms using uploaded identity documents
Detect and explicitly flag conflicts between Central and State scheme versions
We looked at 3 existing approaches first.
Before building anything, we asked — does something like this already exist? Here's what we found:
Citizens rely on local government offices, NGOs, or relatives to discover schemes manually.
Entirely dependent on who you know
Information is often outdated or wrong
Use a general-purpose chatbot (ChatGPT, etc.) to answer questions about government schemes.
No grounding in actual PDF documents
Can't detect Central vs State conflicts
The government's own portal — searchable database of central and some state schemes.
Just a search box — no eligibility check
Every existing approach solves part of the problem. None of them solve all of it — and the gap they leave is exactly what causes that 73% number.
PolicyPilot is the only approach that addresses all five gaps simultaneously.
An agentic AI that reads actual government PDFs, matches citizens to schemes they qualify for, detects policy conflicts, and fills out their applications — all from a plain language description.
Not a search box. Not a generic chatbot. An actual intelligent agent.
A citizen's journey, step by step.
Voice or plain text — no forms, no dropdowns
Aadhaar + income cert — everything else is auto-read
Agent queries RAG pipeline over real scheme PDFs
Conflict engine flags any Central vs State contradictions
EXAMPLE — TRY THIS IN THE DEMO
"I am a 45-year-old farmer in Gujarat, 2 acres of land, income below ₹1.5 lakh."
→  Matched:  PM-KISAN  ·  Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana  ·  Gujarat Kisan Sahay  ·  Soil Health Card Scheme
→  Output:  Full checklist per scheme  ·  PM-KISAN draft form pre-filled  ·  Conflict flag raised on PMFBY (Central vs Gujarat version)
5+ real official govt. scheme PDFs loaded
LLM responds, citing exact PDF clause
→ Both clauses surfaced. Both sources cited. Nothing hidden.
The problem statement says hiding contradictions = immediate DQ. PolicyPilot never hides them.
Output →   Scheme List   ·   Application Checklist   ·   Conflict Flags   ·   Draft Form   ·   Hindi Summary
Stack:  LangChain/LangGraph  ·  GPT-4o  ·  LlamaIndex  ·  FAISS/Pinecone  ·  Whisper  ·  PyMuPDF  ·  FastAPI  ·  React
You say it in your own words — or speak it. No dropdowns, no forms. The agent figures out the rest from what you've said.
Every recommendation traces back to an exact clause in an official government PDF. No guessing, no paraphrasing from memory.
When Central and State versions of the same scheme contradict each other, we flag it explicitly — never silently pick one over the other.
Upload your Aadhaar and income certificate. We extract your details and pre-fill the application form. You just review and submit.
Not just 'you qualify' — we tell you exactly which documents to bring, which office to visit, and what the deadline is for each scheme.
Plain-language summary of all results in Hindi or your preferred regional language — no legal jargon, no English barrier.
Four Things We'll Show You Live
Type or speak a citizen description → agent outputs matched schemes, each with eligibility reasoning and exact PDF clause citations.
Two official PDFs with contradicting criteria → system flags it visibly, cites both sources. Nothing silently resolved.
Upload Aadhaar + income cert → watch the PM-KISAN form fill itself. Citizen only fills in what's not in their documents.
Same results, plain Hindi. No jargon. No assumptions about the citizen's English ability. It just works in their language.
We're not claiming this will fix everything. But if even a fraction of those 73% of citizens start claiming what they're entitled to — that's real, measurable change.
5+ PDFs, working prototype, conflict detection live
50+ schemes, all states, voice input, feedback loop
National rollout, Gram Panchayat kiosks, offline mode
Four of us — one hackathon, one real problem, one weekend.
to access the welfare they deserve."
